baseline.note: ledger totals that predate canonical lifecycle accounting (accounted explicitly)

reconciliation.scope_note: lifecycle counts are cumulative since canonical accounting began; baseline counts are legacy ledger totals that predate it; ledger/gate totals == baseline + accounted.

BTCUSD.indicator_name: Hermes BTC Pulse Composite v4 Orderflow Loose

tradingview.note: TradingView alerts are candidate signals only — they cannot place, resize, or bypass trades; the strategy + execution gate remain the sole trade authority.

edge_vs_5min_outcome.note: observe-only: did the TradingView signal at entry predict the 5-min Chainlink outcome (signal_hit_rate vs baseline_up_rate), and did aligning help the bot win (aligned vs opposed bot_win_rate)? Never affects trading.

rsi_trend.note: observe-only: learns P(next 5-min outcome | RSI alert trend state) from the alert history and scores its own next-window predictions. Never trades.

promotion.note: observe-only diagnostic; eligible buckets are NOT auto-promoted to trading authority unless promotion_allowed_by_config is true AND explicitly wired. The execution gate remains the sole trade authority.

signal_gate.note: when active, a paper trade is taken only if a fresh TradingView signal agrees with the side; it can only PREVENT trades, never force or bypass them.

context_gate.note: hard prior gate: blocks proven-losing entry contexts (volume spikes, noise regime, far-from-resolution) before enough samples exist for the learned selectivity gate. Can only PREVENT trades — never forces, sizes, or bypasses the execution gate, which remains the sole trade authority.

learning.note: the bot's own settled-trade experience (calibrated edge model) adjusts the directional probability; it grows as more trades settle. The execution-quality gate, paper-realism, and reconciliation are unchanged and still veto every trade — learning can never bypass them.

last_note.summary: DOWN_STRONG, range_bottom, lower_wick_rejection, dead volume, and bearish_aligned buckets now show confirmed positive EV with n>=13, win-rates 0.57-0.77 above breakeven, and net positive realized pnl after costs; most other regimes remain noise with negative aggregate pnl of -33.88 USD across 100 trades. UP direction and short ttc (<60s) buckets are clear underperformers. No prior analysis provided so no changes tracked yet.
warnings: All EV_after_cost values positive yet total realized pnl negative indicates unmodeled costs or slippage
n<8 buckets ignored per rules; many remaining buckets still low power
observe_only mode means no live execution risk but also no real fills
focus_next: accumulate more samples on range_bottom + lower_wick_rejection intersection
track DOWN_STRONG stability by hurst and adx
monitor if dead-volume edge persists above breakeven after 50+ additional trades

history.summary: DOWN signals (esp. DOWN_STRONG n=41 wr=0.585 and DOWN_WEAK n=14 wr=0.643) show consistent positive EV and wr above typical breakeven after costs with n>=8; range_bottom, lower_wick_rejection, dead volume, and 60-120s TTC buckets also clear the n>=8 + positive EV threshold while UP and most z-score/HTF buckets remain marginal or negative. Overall realized PnL still negative due to small sample and UP drag, indicating edge is real but narrow and regime-dependent rather than broad. No prior analysis exists so all patterns treated as initial observations.
changes_since_last: No prior analysis; this is baseline

history.summary: DOWN signals (esp. DOWN_WEAK n=15 wr=0.667 and DOWN_STRONG n=43 wr=0.581) in trending regimes, range_bottom, lower_wick_rejection, dead/active volume, and 60-240s TTC show win-rates above breakeven with positive avg_ev_after_cost and n>=8, indicating confirmed edge; UP signals and <60s TTC remain unprofitable with negative realized pnl. Overall sample of 80 trades still shows mixed total pnl despite positive EV metrics, with selectivity heavily favoring DOWN. No material changes possible without prior_analysis.
changes_since_last: First analysis; no prior baseline available

history.summary: DOWN signals (esp. STRONG/WEAK, n=63 total) show confirmed edge with wr 0.58-0.60, positive EV ~0.118 after costs and small positive pnl; range_bottom (n=23, wr 0.739, pnl +34), lower_wick_rejection (n=11), bearish_aligned mtf (n=27), dead/active volume, >=240s/60-120s ttc, and na zscore buckets meet criteria for confirmed profitability while UP and short-ttc buckets remain negative. Overall sample (85 trades) still yields net negative pnl due to UP and adverse regimes outweighing the selective edges. No prior analysis available so all patterns treated as initial observations.

DOWN signals (esp. DOWN_STRONG n=46 wr=0.587 and DOWN_WEAK n=20 wr=0.6) show confirmed positive EV (~0.117) and net pnl after 88 settled trades; UP signals remain unprofitable. Range_bottom, dead/active volume, bearish_aligned MTF, and ttc>=120s buckets meet confirmation thresholds (n>=8, wr>breakeven, +EV); most other slices are noise or under-sampled.

With 93 settled trades, DOWN_STRONG signals (n=51, WR 60.8%) and range_bottom entries (n=27, WR 74.1%) show confirmed positive EV after costs with sufficient samples; DOWN overall outperforms UP materially while short-hold (<60s) and volume-spike buckets remain clearly negative. Most other buckets are still too small or mixed to confirm. Sample remains modest so selectivity on DOWN + favorable regime combinations is the only emerging edge.

DOWN_STRONG/DOWN_WEAK signals in trending regimes with ttc>=120s, range_bottom, lower_wick_rejection, bearish_aligned, dead/active volume, and v4 indicator show win-rates 0.60-0.75 above breakeven with positive EV after costs and n>=20; these are confirmed. UP signals, volume_spike, range_top, bull_close_near_high, and most z-score extremes remain unprofitable or noisy with negative realized pnl despite positive avg_ev. Overall sample of 95 is still small and selective rejection rate extremely high.

With 97 settled trades, DOWN signals (esp. DOWN_STRONG) and a handful of regime buckets show modestly positive realized PnL and win-rates above 0.55-0.60, while UP and most other slices remain negative or too small; avg_ev_after_cost stays positive across buckets but has not yet translated into consistent net profitability. No buckets meet strict confirmation criteria of confidently beating their own breakeven win-rate with material positive EV and n>>50. Sample remains too limited and noisy for live deployment.
changes_since_last: No prior analysis provided; this is baseline view

history.summary: DOWN_STRONG, range_bottom, lower_wick_rejection, dead volume, and bearish_aligned buckets now show confirmed positive EV with n>=13, win-rates 0.57-0.77 above breakeven, and net positive realized pnl after costs; most other regimes remain noise with negative aggregate pnl of -33.88 USD across 100 trades. UP direction and short ttc (<60s) buckets are clear underperformers. No prior analysis provided so no changes tracked yet.

analyst_A.note: observe-only Grok research analyst that LEARNS the bot's trading patterns from its growing settled-trade/selectivity/late-window evidence and refines across cycles (continuity). Diagnostics only — never trades.

predictor_B.note: observe-only Grok P(up) per signal; graded vs realized BTC move before it could ever be trusted; never places/sizes/bypasses a trade.

grok_signal_intel.note: A analyzes signal-learning performance; B predicts P(up) per signal and is graded vs realized moves. Both observe-only — never place/size/bypass a trade; the execution gate remains the sole trade authority.

aggression.note: loosens (more explore/looser exploit/larger size) as acted trades profit; tightens on losses; circuit breaker is the hard floor.

grok_decider.note: Grok decides; bot executes (paper). shadow=decide+grade only (no trade), follow=engine follows direction/size subject to the deterministic floor (execution realism, risk caps, freshness). Fail-closed -> no_trade.

headlines: Bitcoin ETFs see continued outflows (~$68M daily, sustained negative streak)
Crypto market drops as Nasdaq tech selloff spills into digital assets (BTC ~$62k)
50+ crypto leaders meet US senators today urging crypto market structure bill
CLARITY Act field hearing locked for July 17 in NYC
Bitcoin volatility looks cheap ahead of $10B options settlement

verifier.note: independent Claude maker-checker; can only veto/shrink a Grok-proposed paper trade, never force one; fail-open; PAPER ONLY.

last_note.summary: Paper BTC bot is fundamentally unprofitable: 358 settled trades, 52.5% win rate, -$86 PnL, profit factor 0.84, with losses averaging $3.92 vs wins $2.98. No context shows robust edge—most buckets are coin-flip or losing. The market is efficient at 5-min horizons; small sample pockets (zscore -2..-1: n=4, 75% WR; stale_polymarket_down: n=14, 71% WR) are noise, not exploitable signal.

knob_recommendations.reason: conviction <0.2 bucket loses -$23.76 on n=9; filter out low-confidence noise
conviction >=0.8 bucket 0% WR on n=1; extreme confidence unreliable in efficient markets
ttc <60s and 60-120s both lose (50% and 33% WR); require >=120s window to reduce execution noise
chop_noise state: n=18, 38.9% WR, -$21.09 PnL; avoid directionless regimes
stale_polymarket_up: n=3, 0% WR, -$15; contrarian signal fails when poly stale upward

new_lessons.rule: 358 trades, 52.5% WR, -$86 PnL, PF=0.84: no exploitable edge in base strategy; 5-min BTC is near-efficient
zscore -2..-1 (n=4, 75% WR) and stale_polymarket_down (n=14, 71% WR) are small-sample flukes, not robust edges
avg loss $3.92 > avg win $2.98; negative asymmetry compounds losses even at slight sub-50% WR
Brier 0.230 vs baseline 0.250; model barely beats coin-flip, not predictive enough for profitable directionality
regime=trending: n=34, 50% WR, -$21 PnL; trend-following fails at 5-min scale due to lag and noise
execution gate rejected 42 trades as negative_ev_after_slippage; gate is working but pre-gate model still unprofitable
conviction <0.2 loses -$23.76 (22% WR); conviction >=0.8 loses -$5 (0% WR, n=1); middle conviction (0.2-0.8) performs better but still net negative
ttc <120s buckets lose more (33-40% WR) vs 120-240s (51.9% WR); sub-2min window adds execution noise, no edge

research_loop.note: strategy-layer research meta-loop: independent model proposes exploit/avoid contexts + bounded knob nudges + lessons. Observe-only unless auto_apply (whitelisted+bounded). PAPER ONLY.

recent.rule: Conviction <0.2 yields 22% WR, largest loss bucket—model is anti-predictive at low confidence, hard-floor at 0.2+
markov_state=chop_noise: 17 trades, 35% WR, -$24.29 PnL → blacklist or require offsetting signal
ttc <60s: n=4, 25% WR, -$13.06 → execution/slippage overwhelms any model edge
zscore ≤-2 or 1..2: both lose (n=4+2) → momentum continues, mean-reversion bet fails
half_life <30s: n=30, 50% WR, -$15.41 → price oscillates too fast for 5-min resolution edge
355 trades, profit_factor=0.85, avg_loss > avg_win → current feature set does NOT beat market efficiency at 5-min scale
358 trades, 52.5% WR, -$86 PnL, PF=0.84: no exploitable edge in base strategy; 5-min BTC is near-efficient
zscore -2..-1 (n=4, 75% WR) and stale_polymarket_down (n=14, 71% WR) are small-sample flukes, not robust edges
Brier 0.230 vs baseline 0.250; model barely beats coin-flip, not predictive enough for profitable directionality
regime=trending: n=34, 50% WR, -$21 PnL; trend-following fails at 5-min scale due to lag and noise
execution gate rejected 42 trades as negative_ev_after_slippage; gate is working but pre-gate model still unprofitable
ttc <120s buckets lose more (33-40% WR) vs 120-240s (51.9% WR); sub-2min window adds execution noise, no edge

loops.note: formalized sub-loops (data/signal/verify/execute/risk/research/news/lessons); each has a trigger + skill + verifier + verifiable stop. PAPER ONLY.

promotion.note: observe-only diagnostic; eligible buckets are NOT auto-promoted to trading authority unless promotion_allowed_by_config is true AND explicitly wired. The execution gate remains the sole trade authority.

learned_selectivity_gate.note: rejects ONLY buckets confidently below their own breakeven win-rate (Wilson upper bound < breakeven AND net-negative PnL); coin-flip / not-significant buckets are NOT rejected. Can only make the bot MORE selective — never trades, resizes, or bypasses the execution gate. Exploration tracked separately.

counterfactual.note: in-sample replay using final accumulated bucket evidence (diagnostic estimate)

gate.note: when enabled, only late-window AND high-conviction setups may trade; can only PREVENT trades, never force/size/bypass the execution gate.

edge_measurement.note: observe-only: grades whether late-window high-conviction trades win more (cohort vs other) from live settled trades; never affects trading.
